arabic: وَمَن يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُ مَخْرَجًا وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ
translation: Whoever fears Allah, He will make for him a way out, and provide from where he does not expect.
tafsir: This ayah promises that taqwa (God-consciousness) leads to divine provision and relief from difficulty.
reflectionTemplate: Your {weakest_area} needs attention. Taqwa — fear and awareness of Allah — is the key. Prioritize your connection with Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this ayah with someone anxious about their future. Allah's provision comes from where we least expect.

arabic: فَإِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا إِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا
translation: For indeed, with hardship comes ease. Indeed, with hardship comes ease.
tafsir: Allah promises ease after every difficulty — mentioned twice for emphasis. Hardship is temporary; ease is guaranteed.
reflectionTemplate: You are going through difficulty, but Allah promises ease — not once, but twice. Hold on.
dawahTemplate: Someone around you is struggling. Remind them: ease is not just possible — it is promised by Allah.

arabic: إِنَّ فِي خَلْقِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَاخْتِلَافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ لَآيَاتٍ لِّأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ
translation: Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and earth, and the alternation of night and day, are signs for those of understanding.
tafsir: This ayah invites reflection on creation — finding Allah in the patterns of the universe is the essence of taqwa.
reflectionTemplate: Your time barakah is low and your taqwa was distracted. This ayah invites you to LOOK — to find Allah in the patterns of your day.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels life is random. The signs are everywhere — they just need someone to point at them.

arabic: لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا
translation: Allah does not burden a soul beyond that it can bear.
tafsir: Every test is calibrated to your capacity. What you face, you can handle — with Allah's help.
reflectionTemplate: Whatever you are facing, Allah knows you can handle it. He does not burden you beyond your capacity.
dawahTemplate: Remind someone overwhelmed by life: Allah, who created them, knows exactly what they can bear.

arabic: فَاذْكُرُونِي أَذْكُرْكُمْ وَاشْكُرُوا لِي وَلَا تَكْفُرُونِ
translation: So remember Me; I will remember you. And be grateful to Me and do not deny Me.
tafsir: Dhikr (remembrance of Allah) is a two-way connection — when you remember Him, He remembers you with His mercy and blessings.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa is low because you have forgotten to remember Him. Start with even one dhikr — He will remember you back.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels disconnected from Allah. The connection starts with just one moment of remembrance.

arabic: قُلْ يَا عِبَادِيَ الَّذِينَ أَسْرَفُوا عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِهِمْ لَا تَقْنَطُوا مِن رَّحْمَةِ اللَّهِ
translation: Say: O My servants who have transgressed against themselves, do not despair of the mercy of Allah.
tafsir: Allah addresses the sinners directly — His mercy is greater than any sin. Despair is never the answer.
reflectionTemplate: You may feel you have gone too far, but Allah's mercy is infinite. Do not despair — turn back to Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks they are too sinful for Allah's mercy. He calls them 'My servants' — not sinners.

arabic: وَمَا وَدَّعَكَ رَبُّكَ وَمَا قَلَىٰ وَلَلْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لَّكَ مِنَ الْأُولَىٰ
translation: Your Lord has not forsaken you, nor has He become displeased. And the Hereafter is better for you than the first life.
tafsir: Revealed during a period of silence in revelation — Allah reassures the Prophet ﷺ that He has not abandoned him.
reflectionTemplate: If you feel Allah has forgotten you, He has not. The best is yet to come — in this life and the next.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone going through a silent, difficult season. Allah has not forsaken them.

arabic: أَلَا بِذِكْرِ اللَّهِ تَطْمَئِنُّ الْقُلُوبُ
translation: Verily, in the remembrance of Allah do hearts find rest.
tafsir: True peace comes not from circumstances but from connection with the Creator. Dhikr is the medicine for restless hearts.
reflectionTemplate: Your heart is restless because it is disconnected from its source of peace. Return to dhikr — even quietly.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone searching for inner peace. The answer has been there all along — remembrance of Allah.

arabic: قُلْ إِنَّ صَلَاتِي وَنُسُكِي وَمَحْيَايَ وَمَمَاتِي لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
translation: Say: Indeed, my prayer, my rites of sacrifice, my living and my dying are for Allah, Lord of the worlds.
tafsir: This is the ultimate declaration of purpose — everything we do is for Allah. Salah is the anchor of this purpose.
reflectionTemplate: Your prayer consistency reflects your connection to purpose. Every salah is a meeting with the One who created you.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who asks why you pray. Your salah is not a ritual — it is a declaration of purpose.

arabic: اتْلُ مَا أُوحِيَ إِلَيْكَ مِنَ الْكِتَابِ وَأَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ إِنَّ الصَّلَاةَ تَنْهَىٰ عَنِ الْفَحْشَاءِ وَالْمُنكَرِ
translation: Recite what has been revealed to you of the Book and establish prayer. Indeed, prayer prohibits immorality and wrongdoing.
tafsir: Salah is not just worship — it is a shield against sin. When prayer is strong, character is protected.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa is low and your salah needs focus. Strengthen your prayer — it will protect you from what pulls you away.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling with sin. The solution starts with prayer — it is a built-in protection system.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الْمُزَّمِّلُ قُمِ اللَّيْلَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا نِّصْفَهُ أَوِ انقُصْ مِنْهُ قَلِيلًا أَوْ زِدْ عَلَيْهِ وَرَتِّلِ الْقُرْآنَ تَرْتِيلًا
translation: O you who wraps himself, arise to pray at night, except for a little — half of it or slightly less, or add to it, and recite the Quran with measured recitation.
tafsir: Tahajjud is the prayer of the closest servants to Allah. Even a little night worship transforms the soul.
reflectionTemplate: Your heart is restless. The cure is in the last third of the night — even two rak'ahs of tahajjud can change everything.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels spiritually disconnected. The night prayer is the secret weapon of the believers.

arabic: وَعِبَادُ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الَّذِينَ يَمْشُونَ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ هَوْنًا وَإِذَا خَاطَبَهُمُ الْجَاهِلُونَ قَالُوا سَلَامًا وَالَّذِينَ يَبِيتُونَ لِرَبِّهِمْ سُجَّدًا وَقِيَامًا
translation: And the servants of the Most Merciful are those who walk upon the earth humbly, and when the ignorant address them, they say words of peace. And those who spend the night prostrating and standing before their Lord.
tafsir: The servants of Ar-Rahman are defined by humility, patience with ignorance, and night worship — a complete spiritual profile.
reflectionTemplate: Your nafs awareness shows you know your struggles. Tonight, try one sajdah in the night — be among the servants of Ar-Rahman.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who wants to know what a true Muslim looks like. This ayah paints the complete picture.

arabic: وَمَا خَلَقْتُ الْجِنَّ وَالْإِنسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ
translation: And I did not create the jinn and mankind except to worship Me.
tafsir: The purpose of existence is worship. Everything else — career, family, wealth — is the means, not the end.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation needs strengthening. Remember your core purpose: you were created to worship Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone searching for life's purpose. The answer is in the very reason they exist.

arabic: الْمَالُ وَالْبَنُونَ زِينَةُ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَالْبَاقِيَاتُ الصَّالِحَاتُ خَيْرٌ عِندَ رَبِّكَ ثَوَابًا وَخَيْرٌ أَمَلًا
translation: Wealth and children are the adornment of the life of this world. But the enduring good deeds are better with your Lord for reward and better for hope.
tafsir: Dunya is temporary decoration — the lasting investments are good deeds that carry forward to the akhirah.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation is low. Dunya is beautiful but temporary. Invest in what lasts — good deeds, not just wealth.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone chasing only worldly success. The real investment is in what endures beyond this life.

arabic: وَالْعَصْرِ إِنَّ الْإِنسَانَ لَفِي خُسْرٍ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالْحَقِّ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالصَّبْرِ
translation: By time, indeed mankind is in loss, except for those who believe and do righteous deeds and advise each other to truth and advise each other to patience.
tafsir: Allah swears by time — every moment is either gain or loss. The formula: faith + action + truth + patience = salvation.
reflectionTemplate: Your time barakah is low. Time is either gaining or losing — there is no standing still. Invest your hours wisely.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels they are wasting their life. The formula for success is right here in 3 ayahs.

arabic: وَإِذَا سَأَلَكَ عِبَادِي عَنِّي فَإِنِّي قَرِيبٌ ۖ أُجِيبُ دَعْوَةَ الدَّاعِ إِذَا دَعَانِ
translation: And when My servants ask you about Me — indeed I am near. I respond to the invocation of the supplicant when he calls upon Me.
tafsir: Allah is near — closer than we think. He responds to every dua, though the answer may come in unexpected forms.
reflectionTemplate: Your duas may feel unanswered, but Allah is near. He responds — sometimes differently than we expect, but always with wisdom.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks Allah is far away. He says 'I am near' — the distance is from our side, not His.

arabic: وَقَالَ رَبُّكُمُ ادْعُونِي أَسْتَجِبْ لَكُمْ
translation: And your Lord says: Call upon Me; I will respond to you.
tafsir: Allah commands us to make dua — and promises to respond. The act of calling upon Him is itself an act of worship.
reflectionTemplate: You have active duas waiting. Keep calling — Allah has commanded you to ask, and He has promised to respond.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who has stopped making dua. Allah Himself is asking them to call upon Him.

arabic: لَئِن شَكَرْتُمْ لَأَزِيدَنَّكُمْ
translation: If you are grateful, I will surely increase you.
tafsir: Gratitude is the multiplier of blessings. The more you thank Allah, the more He gives — in rizq, peace, and barakah.
reflectionTemplate: Your rizq barakah is strong — Allah has blessed you. Increase in shukr, and He will increase you further.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who wants more from life. The secret is not working harder — it is being more grateful.

arabic: إِنَّ الْأَبْرَارَ يَشْرَبُونَ مِن كَأْسٍ كَانَ مِزَاجُهَا كَافُورًا عَيْنًا يَشْرَبُ بِهَا عِبَادُ اللَّهِ يُفَجِّرُونَهَا تَفْجِيرًا
translation: Indeed, the righteous will drink from a cup whose mixture is of Kafur, a spring from which the servants of Allah will drink; they will make it gush forth in abundance.
tafsir: The reward of the righteous in Jannah is beyond imagination — rivers of pure, delicious drink flowing eternally.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation needs a boost. The reward waiting for you in Jannah is beyond anything this world can offer.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who is attached to worldly pleasures. The real enjoyment is in the hereafter.

arabic: مَنْ عَمِلَ صَالِحًا مِّن ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنثَىٰ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَنُحْيِيَنَّهُ حَيَاةً طَيِّبَةً
translation: Whoever does righteousness, whether male or female, while being a believer — We will surely cause them to live a good life.
tafsir: The promise of a 'good life' (hayatan tayyibah) is tied to faith + action — not wealth or status.
reflectionTemplate: Your barakah scores show where life feels restricted. A good life comes from righteous action, not just circumstances.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who equates a good life with wealth. Allah defines the good life differently — through faith and action.

arabic: كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ذَائِقَةُ الْمَوْتِ ۗ وَإِنَّمَا تُوَفَّوْنَ أُجُورَكُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ
translation: Every soul will taste death. And you will only be given your full compensation on the Day of Resurrection.
tafsir: Death is certain — the real account is on Judgment Day. This life is temporary; the akhirah is forever.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation is low. Death is certain — every day is one day closer. Prepare for the real life.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone living as if they will never die. The reminder of death puts everything in perspective.

arabic: اعْلَمُوا أَنَّمَا الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ وَزِينَةٌ وَتَفَاخُرٌ بَيْنَكُمْ وَتَكَاثُرٌ فِي الْأَمْوَالِ وَالْأَوْلَادِ
translation: Know that the life of this world is but amusement and diversion and adornment and boasting to one another and competition in increase of wealth and children.
tafsir: Allah defines dunya precisely — it is play, decoration, and competition. The real life is the akhirah.
reflectionTemplate: You are competing in dunya — wealth, status, comparison. Remember: this is play. The real competition is for Jannah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone consumed by worldly competition. Allah Himself calls it 'play and diversion.'

arabic: وَأْمُرْ أَهْلَكَ بِالصَّلَاةِ وَاصْطَبِرْ عَلَيْهَا
translation: And enjoin prayer upon your family and be steadfast therein.
tafsir: Salah is a family responsibility — leading by example and encouraging those you love to pray.
reflectionTemplate: Your prayer consistency affects not just you but your family. Be the one who leads them to salah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with a parent struggling to get their family to pray. Leadership starts at home.

arabic: وَأَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ طَرَفَيِ النَّهَارِ وَزُلَفًا مِّنَ اللَّيْلِ ۚ إِنَّ الْحَسَنَاتِ يُذْهِبْنَ السَّيِّئَاتِ
translation: And establish prayer at the two ends of the day and at the approach of the night. Indeed, good deeds remove bad deeds.
tafsir: The five prayers are distributed throughout the day — each one erases sins and elevates your rank.
reflectionTemplate: Your prayer consistency needs work. Each salah erases the sins before it — do not miss this mercy.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels weighed down by sins. The solution is in the five daily prayers.

arabic: إِنَّ الصَّلَاةَ كَانَتْ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ كِتَابًا مَّوْقُوتًا
translation: Indeed, prayer has been decreed upon the believers a decree of specified times.
tafsir: Salah is not optional — it is an obligation with specific times. Praying on time is a sign of true faith.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa pulse is low. Salah at its proper time reconnects you with Allah — do not delay it.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who prays whenever they feel like it. Salah has appointed times — respect them.

arabic: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الَّذِينَ هُمْ فِي صَلَاتِهِمْ خَاشِعُونَ
translation: Certainly will the believers have succeeded: They who are during their prayer humbly submissive.
tafsir: Success is defined by khushu in prayer — not just performing the movements but having a humble, present heart.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa is low — your prayers may be happening but without khushu. Focus on being present in your next salah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks prayer is just physical movements. True success is in the humility of the heart.

arabic: أَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ لِدُلُوكِ الشَّمْسِ إِلَىٰ غَسَقِ اللَّيْلِ وَقُرْآنَ الْفَجْرِ ۖ إِنَّ قُرْآنَ الْفَجْرِ كَانَ مَشْهُودًا
translation: Establish prayer at the decline of the sun until the darkness of the night and the Quran of dawn. Indeed, the recitation of dawn is ever witnessed.
tafsir: Fajr prayer is witnessed by the angels — it has a special status. Waking for Fajr is a sign of strong iman.
reflectionTemplate: Your fajr consistency matters more than you know. The angels witness your dawn prayer — do not miss this blessed time.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who struggles with Fajr. The dawn prayer is witnessed by angels — it is special.

arabic: وَاسْتَعِينُوا بِالصَّبْرِ وَالصَّلَاةِ ۚ وَإِنَّهَا لَكَبِيرَةٌ إِلَّا عَلَى الْخَاشِعِينَ
translation: And seek help through patience and prayer, and indeed, it is difficult except for the humbly submissive.
tafsir: Patience and prayer are the two tools for every difficulty. Prayer is hard only for those without khushu.
reflectionTemplate: You are facing difficulty. The tools are patience and prayer — but only if your heart is humble in salah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone going through hardship. The two solutions Allah gives: patience and prayer.

arabic: وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتُ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءُ بَعْضٍ ۚ يَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ
translation: The believing men and believing women are allies of one another. They enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong.
tafsir: Believers are allies — they support each other in good and protect each other from evil. Brotherhood in deen.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen needs strengthening. Be an ally to other believers — enjoin good, forbid evil, and support each other.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks Islam is a solo journey. Believers are allies — we are stronger together.

arabic: إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ إِخْوَةٌ
translation: The believers are but brothers.
tafsir: Brotherhood in faith is a fundamental bond — stronger than blood ties in many ways. It is built on shared faith.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah readiness is low. Start with your Muslim brothers and sisters — the bond of faith is the strongest bond.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone feeling isolated. The believers are brothers — find your community.

arabic: ادْعُ إِلَىٰ سَبِيلِ رَبِّكَ بِالْحِكْمَةِ وَالْمَوْعِظَةِ الْحَسَنَةِ
translation: Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good instruction.
tafsir: Dawah is done with wisdom and beautiful preaching — not force, not argument, but gentle invitation.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah readiness needs growth. Start small — invite with wisdom and good character, not lectures.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who is afraid of giving dawah. It is not about being a scholar — it is about wisdom and beauty.

arabic: وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ قَوْلًا مِّمَّن دَعَا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَعَمِلَ صَالِحًا وَقَالَ إِنَّنِي مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ
translation: And who is better in speech than one who invites to Allah and does righteousness and says: Indeed, I am of the Muslims.
tafsir: The best speech is dawah — inviting to Allah with good deeds and declaring your identity as a Muslim.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah potential is untapped. You do not need to be a scholar — just invite with good deeds and sincerity.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who wants to make a difference. The best speech is inviting others to Allah.

arabic: وَالْعَصْرِ إِنَّ الْإِنسَانَ لَفِي خُسْرٍ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالْحَقِّ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالصَّبْرِ
translation: By time, indeed mankind is in loss, except for those who believe and do righteous deeds and advise each other to truth and patience.
tafsir: Faith, action, truth, and patience — these four together save a person from loss. Missing any one is dangerous.
reflectionTemplate: Your sabr is being tested. Hold on to patience — it is one of the four things that save you from loss.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels like they are losing at life. The formula is right here: faith, action, truth, patience.

arabic: وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُم بِشَيْءٍ مِّنَ الْخَوْفِ وَالْجُوعِ وَنَقْصٍ مِّنَ الْأَمْوَالِ وَالْأَنفُسِ وَالثَّمَرَاتِ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ الصَّابِرِينَ الَّذِينَ إِذَا أَصَابَتْهُم مُّصِيبَةٌ قَالُوا إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ أُولَٰئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ صَلَوَاتٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَرَحْمَةٌ ۖ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُهْتَدُونَ
translation: And We will surely test you with something of fear and hunger and a loss of wealth and lives and fruits, but give good tidings to the patient who, when disaster strikes, say: Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return. Those are the ones upon whom are blessings from their Lord and mercy, and it is those who are the guided.
tafsir: Tests are guaranteed — fear, hunger, loss. But the patient ones who say 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un' receive blessings, mercy, and guidance.
reflectionTemplate: You are being tested. The response of the patient is 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un' — and with that comes blessings and mercy.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in grief. The response to loss is not despair — it is 'We belong to Allah and to Him we return.'

arabic: إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِرَ اللَّهُ وَجِلَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَإِذَا تُلِيَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُهُ زَادَتْهُمْ إِيمَانًا وَعَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ الَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ الصَّلَاةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَاهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ حَقًّا
translation: The believers are only those who, when Allah is mentioned, their hearts become fearful, and when His verses are recited to them, it increases them in faith; and upon their Lord they rely. The ones who establish prayer, and from what We have provided them, they spend. Those are the believers, true believers.
tafsir: True faith is defined by fear of Allah, growing iman, tawakkul, salah, and generosity — a complete spiritual profile.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa pulse shows your God-consciousness. When Allah is mentioned, does your heart tremble? That is the sign of true faith.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who wonders what true faith looks like. This ayah gives the complete picture.

arabic: مَا أَصَابَ مِن مُّصِيبَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۗ وَمَن يُؤْمِن بِاللَّهِ يَهْدِ قَلْبَهُ
translation: No disaster strikes except by the permission of Allah. And whoever believes in Allah — He will guide his heart.
tafsir: Everything happens by Allah's will. Faith in this truth guides the heart through any difficulty.
reflectionTemplate: Your qadr perception needs strengthening. Nothing happens randomly — everything is by Allah's permission. Trust His wisdom.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who blames luck or fate. Everything is by Allah's permission — and faith guides the heart.

arabic: وَعِندَهُ مَفَاتِحُ الْغَيْبِ لَا يَعْلَمُهَا إِلَّا هُوَ
translation: And with Him are the keys of the unseen; none knows them except Him.
tafsir: Only Allah knows the unseen — the future, the hidden wisdom behind events. Trusting Him means accepting what we cannot see.
reflectionTemplate: Your qadr perception is off. You are trying to see what only Allah can see. Trust His knowledge — He has the keys to the unseen.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone anxious about the future. Only Allah knows what is coming — trust the One who holds the keys.

arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عِندَهُ عِلْمُ السَّاعَةِ وَيُنَزِّلُ الْغَيْثَ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا فِي الْأَرْحَامِ
translation: Indeed, Allah has knowledge of the Hour and sends down rain and knows what is in the wombs.
tafsir: Allah alone knows the unseen — the Hour, the rain, what is in the womb. Human knowledge is limited; His is infinite.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation needs a reset. The Hour is coming — only Allah knows when. Prepare as if it is tomorrow.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks they can predict or control everything. Only Allah knows the unseen.

arabic: وَلَوْ بَسَطَ اللَّهُ الرِّزْقَ لِعِبَادِهِ لَبَغَوْا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَٰكِن يُنَزِّلُ بِقَدَرٍ مَّا يَشَاءُ
translation: And if Allah had extended provision to His servants, they would have transgressed on earth. But He sends down what He wills in a measured way.
tafsir: Allah gives rizq in measured amounts — not because He cannot give more, but because unrestricted wealth leads to corruption.
reflectionTemplate: Your rizq barakah is low. But Allah gives what you need — not always what you want. Trust His distribution.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone complaining about their provision. Allah gives in measure — for your own protection.

arabic: وَجَعَلْنَا اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهَارَ آيَتَيْنِ ۖ فَمَحَوْنَا آيَةَ اللَّيْلِ وَجَعَلْنَا آيَةَ النَّهَارِ مُبْصِرَةً وَكُلَّ إِنسَانٍ أَلْزَمْنَاهُ طَائِرَهُ فِي عُنُقِهِ
translation: And We have made the night and day two signs. Then We removed the sign of the night and made the sign of the day visible, that you may seek bounty from your Lord and know the number of years and account.
tafsir: Day and night are signs from Allah — each has its purpose. The cycle of time reminds us of Allah's wisdom and our accountability.
reflectionTemplate: Your time barakah is low. Day and night are signs — use them wisely. Every hour is an amanah from Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who wastes time. Day and night are signs — each hour has a purpose.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّسْ أَنتُمُ الْفُقَرَاءُ إِلَى اللَّهِ ۖ وَاللَّهُ هُوَ الْغَنِيُّ الْحَمِيدُ
translation: O mankind, you are those in need of Allah, while Allah is the Free of need, the Praiseworthy.
tafsir: Every human is in need of Allah — He is the Self-Sufficient. Our poverty to Him is absolute; His richness is absolute.
reflectionTemplate: Your barakah scores show areas of need. Remember: you are in need of Allah in everything. Turn to Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks they are self-sufficient. Every human is in need of Allah — He is the Free of need.

arabic: وَإِذَا أَذَقْنَا النَّاسَ رَحْمَةً فَرِحُوا بِهَا ۖ وَإِن تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ فَإِنَّ الْإِنسَانَ كَفُورٌ
translation: And when We let the people taste mercy, they rejoice therein, but if evil afflicts them for what their hands have put forth, immediately they despair.
tafsir: Humans tend to be grateful in ease and despair in difficulty. True faith maintains gratitude and patience in both states.
reflectionTemplate: Your sabr and shukr need balance. Be grateful in ease and patient in difficulty — do not let circumstances define your iman.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone whose faith depends on circumstances. True faith is consistent in both ease and hardship.

arabic: وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُمْ حَتَّىٰ نَعْلَمَ الْمُجَاهِدِينَ مِنكُمْ وَالصَّابِرِينَ وَنَبْلُوَّ أَخْبَارَكُمْ
translation: And We will surely test you until We make evident those who strive among you and the patient, and We will test your affairs.
tafsir: Tests reveal who truly strives and who is patient. Allah tests to elevate, not to punish.
reflectionTemplate: You are being tested. This test is revealing your true level — strive and be patient. The reward is with Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels tested beyond measure. Tests reveal the mujahid and the patient — be among them.

arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَظْلِمُ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ ۖ وَإِن تَكُ حَسَنَةً يُضَاعِفْهَا
translation: Indeed, Allah does not do injustice, even as much as an atom's weight. And if there is a good deed, He multiplies it.
tafsir: Allah's justice is absolute — not an atom's weight of injustice. And good deeds are multiplied — His generosity exceeds His justice.
reflectionTemplate: Your fahm shows concerns about fairness. Allah does not do injustice — and He multiplies every good deed. Trust His justice.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who questions divine justice. Allah does not do injustice — even an atom's weight of it.

arabic: وَهُوَ اللَّهُ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَفِي الْأَرْضِ ۖ يَعْلَمُ سِرَّكُمْ وَجَهْرَكُمْ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا تَكْسِبُونَ
translation: And He is Allah in the heavens and on earth. He knows your secret and what you declare, and He knows what you earn.
tafsir: Allah knows everything — your secrets, your public actions, your earnings. Nothing is hidden from Him.
reflectionTemplate: Your nafs awareness is low. Allah knows what you hide and what you show. Be honest with yourself — He already knows.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks they can hide. Allah knows everything — secrets and public actions alike.

arabic: وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنسَانَ وَنَعْلَمُ مَا تُوَسْوِسُ بِهِ نَفْسُهُ ۖ وَنَحْنُ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ حَبْلِ الْوَرِيدِ
translation: And We have already created man and know what his soul whispers to him, and We are closer to him than his jugular vein.
tafsir: Allah knows every whisper of your nafs — and He is closer to you than your own blood. The distance is from your side, not His.
reflectionTemplate: Your nafs awareness shows you know your struggles. Allah knows every whisper — and He is closer than your jugular vein.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels far from Allah. He is closer than your jugular vein — the distance is from your side.

arabic: أَيَحْسَبُ الْإِنسَانُ أَن يُتْرَكَ سُدًى
translation: Does man think that he will be left neglected?
tafsir: Every human will be questioned and accounted for. No one is left without purpose or accountability.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation is low. You will not be left neglected — you will be questioned about every moment.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who lives without purpose. No human is left neglected — every moment is accountable.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الْإِنسَانُ مَا غَرَّكَ بِرَبِّكَ الْكَرِيمِ الَّذِي خَلَقَكَ فَسَوَّاكَ فَعَدَلَكَ فِي أَيِّ صُورَةٍ مَّا شَاءَ رَكَّبَكَ
translation: O mankind, what has deceived you concerning your Lord, the Generous, who created you, proportioned you, and balanced you? In whatever form He willed, He assembled you.
tafsir: Allah asks what deceived you about His generosity. He created you perfectly — how can you doubt His care?
reflectionTemplate: Your barakah perception is distorted. What deceived you about your Lord? He created you perfectly — trust His care.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who doubts Allah's care. He created you perfectly — what deceived you about His generosity?

arabic: وَأَن لَّيْسَ لِلْإِنسَانِ إِلَّا مَا سَعَىٰ وَأَنَّ سَعْيَهُ سَوْفَ يُرَىٰ
translation: And that there is nothing for man except what he strives for, and that his effort will be seen.
tafsir: Every effort counts — nothing is wasted. What you strive for in this life will be seen and rewarded.
reflectionTemplate: Your ilm attitude shows you know what to do. Now strive — every effort will be seen and rewarded by Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks effort is wasted. Nothing is wasted — every strive will be seen and rewarded.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ارْكَعُوا وَاسْجُدُوا وَاعْبُدُوا رَبَّكُمْ وَافْعَلُوا الْخَيْرَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
translation: O you who have believed, bow and prostrate and worship your Lord and do good that you may succeed.
tafsir: Success comes from worship (bowing, prostrating) AND doing good — faith and action together.
reflectionTemplate: Your ilm attitude is growing. Combine knowledge with action — bow, prostrate, worship, and do good.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who knows but does not act. Knowledge without action is incomplete — bow and do good.

arabic: قُلْ إِنَّمَا أَنَا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ يُوحَىٰ إِلَيَّ أَنَّمَا إِلَٰهُكُمْ إِلَٰهٌ وَاحِدٌ
translation: Say: I am only a man like you, to whom it has been revealed that your god is one God.
tafsir: The Prophet ﷺ was human — the message is for all humans. Islam is accessible to everyone, not just the elite.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah readiness needs growth. The Prophet ﷺ was human like you — your dawah starts with being relatable.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks Islam is only for the 'religious.' The Prophet ﷺ was human — the message is for everyone.

arabic: وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً لِّلْعَالَمِينَ
translation: And We have not sent you, O Muhammad, except as a mercy to the worlds.
tafsir: The Prophet ﷺ was sent as mercy — not punishment, not force. Islam is mercy to all creation.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah approach needs softening. The Prophet ﷺ was mercy — your dawah should be mercy too.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks Islam is harsh. The Prophet ﷺ was sent as mercy to all worlds — not punishment.

arabic: إِنَّمَا أَمْرُهُ إِذَا أَرَادَ شَيْئًا أَن يَقُولَ لَهُ كُن فَيَكُونُ
translation: His command is only when He intends a thing that He says to it, 'Be,' and it is.
tafsir: Allah's power is absolute — He creates by saying 'Be.' Nothing is difficult for Him.
reflectionTemplate: Your qadr perception needs this reminder. Nothing is difficult for Allah — He says 'Be' and it is. Trust His power.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks their situation is impossible. For Allah, nothing is impossible — He says 'Be' and it is.

arabic: الَّذِي لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَلَمْ يَتَّخِذْ وَلَدًا وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُ شَرِيكٌ فِي الْمُلْكِ
translation: He to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and who has not taken a son and has not had a partner in dominion.
tafsir: Allah owns everything — alone, without partner. All dominion belongs to Him.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation needs this truth. Allah owns everything — your wealth, your health, your life. It all belongs to Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone attached to worldly possessions. Everything belongs to Allah — we are just temporary custodians.

arabic: فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطَىٰ وَاتَّقَىٰ وَصَدَّقَ بِالْحُسْنَىٰ فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ لِلْيُسْرَىٰ
translation: As for he who gives and fears Allah and believes in the best reward, We will ease him toward ease.
tafsir: Generosity + taqwa + belief in good = ease from Allah. The path to ease is through giving and fearing Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your barakah is connected to your generosity and taqwa. Give, fear Allah, and believe in the best — ease will come.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling with barakah. The formula: give, fear Allah, believe in the best — ease follows.

arabic: هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَرْضَ ذَلُولًا فَامْشُوا فِي مَنَاكِبِهَا وَكُلُوا مِن رِّزْقِهِ
translation: It is He who made the earth tame for you, so walk among its slopes and eat of His provision.
tafsir: The earth is made for you — walk, explore, eat from Allah's provision. He created it all for your benefit.
reflectionTemplate: Your health barakah is low but the earth is made for you. Walk, move, explore — your body is an amanah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels trapped by health issues. The earth is made for you — walk and eat from His provision.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّسُ قَدْ جَاءَتْكُم مَّوْعِظَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَشِفَاءٌ لِّمَا فِي الصُّدُورِ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
translation: O mankind, there has come to you instruction from your Lord and healing for what is in the hearts and guidance and mercy for the believers.
tafsir: The Quran is a healing for hearts — not just a book to read but medicine for the soul. Guidance and mercy for believers.
reflectionTemplate: Your heart is restless. The Quran is the healing — even one ayah can bring peace. Read tonight.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone with a troubled heart. The Quran is the healing — not just for knowledge but for the heart.

arabic: وَنُنَزِّلُ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ مَا هُوَ شِفَاءٌ وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
translation: And We send down of the Quran that which is healing and mercy for the believers.
tafsir: The Quran itself is mercy and healing — its recitation brings peace, its understanding brings guidance.
reflectionTemplate: You are disconnected from the Quran. It is your healing and mercy — do not deprive yourself of it.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling emotionally. The Quran is healing and mercy — start with one ayah tonight.

arabic: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَن تَزَكَّىٰ وَذَكَرَ اسْمَ رَبِّهِ فَصَلَّىٰ بَلْ تُؤْثِرُونَ الْحَيَاةَ الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقَىٰ
translation: He has certainly succeeded who purifies himself and remembers the name of his Lord and prays. But you prefer the worldly life, while the Hereafter is better and more enduring.
tafsir: Success is in purification, remembrance, and prayer. But humans prefer dunya — while akhirah is better and lasting.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation is low. You prefer dunya, but akhirah is better and more lasting. Shift your focus.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone chasing only this life. The hereafter is better and more enduring — do not trade it for dunya.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَقُولُوا قَوْلًا سَدِيدًا يُصْلِحْ لَكُمْ أَعْمَالَكُمْ
translation: O you who have believed, fear Allah and speak words of appropriate justice. He will amend for you your deeds.
tafsir: Taqwa + truthful speech = reformed deeds. What you say reflects your God-consciousness.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen needs work. Fear Allah and speak truthfully — He will fix your deeds through your words.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who struggles with speech. Fear Allah and speak truth — He will fix everything else.
